published_prior_art.cite: Read2022 sign/zero algorithms and Guay2021 higher-moment rank tests do not prove perturbation stability of this support gap

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Flagship comparison cites Wolf2022, PlagborgMollerWolf2021, and DrautzburgWright2023 without section/page/equation anchors tying them to the tau_w^*(P) frontier.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The Read2022 and Guay2021 comparators are cited only at paper level; the proposed perturbation-stability gap needs location-level anchors.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The claim Delta_w(P)>0 iff M_w(P) is nonempty is false as stated: a non-SVAR LP maximizer can tie an SVAR maximizer at the same support value, making M_w(P) nonempty but Delta_w(P)=0 unless g_w>0 or a no-tie condition is built into M_w.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine the exposed masquerading set as M_w^+(P)={Q in argmax_LP F_w: Q notin Q_SVAR, g_w(Q;P)>0} or add an explicit no-support-tie assumption, then restate Theorem 1 and the parent-reduction corollary.

Add location-level anchors for the flagship comparator chain: Wolf2022 shock-masquerading passage, the Plagborg-Moller-Wolf common-restriction equivalence implication, and the Drautzburg-Wright independence-refinement/nonconvex-set result.

Make tau_w^*(P) operational by specifying the finite polynomial or constrained-optimization problem, including dimension, variables, and constraints, and relate it explicitly to Read-style sign/zero feasibility algorithms.

recommended_next_step: Patch Theorem 1 by excluding support ties, add comparator anchors, and rerun Stage -0.5 on the same mechanism-upgrade kernel.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The proposal does isolate the parent chi_w(P) gap into sign exposure, cumulant-label filtering, and tau_w^*(P), but the setup theorem currently states a false iff without a positive-advantage/no-tie condition.
